content:
You are given the following task to perform in Dafny:

Write a method that checks whether two arrays have the same elements in some order, ignoring duplicates.

The signature should be:

p_6_10_same_set_seq(first: seq<int>, second: seq<int>) returns (are_same_set: bool)

The method should respect the following contract:

ensures are_same_set == (array_to_set(first) == array_to_set(second))

The contract uses the following dafny code:

function array_to_set(arr: seq<int>): set<int>
{
  if |arr| == 0 then {}
  else {arr[0]} + array_to_set(arr[1..])
}

These function(s) must not be used in your implementation of the method.

Produce and show only the Dafny body of this method, including the curly braces that surround it. Do not show the signature nor contract. You must not use 'assume' nor 'expect' anywhere in your code.
{"method_body":"{\n  are_same_set := (forall x: int :: x in first <==> x in second);\n}"}

When using dafny resolve, the below error is emitted and resolve fails:

problem_data/p_6_10_same_set_seq/program.dfy(10,19): Error: quantifiers in non-ghost contexts must be compilable, but Dafny's heuristics can't figure out how to produce or compile a bounded set of values for 'x'
   |
10 |   are_same_set := (forall x: int :: x in first <==> x in second);
   |                    ^^^^^^

1 resolution/type errors detected in program.dfy

Correct the error in code by altering only the method body. Produce and show only the Dafny body, including the curly braces that surround it. Do not show the signature nor contract. You must not use 'assume' nor 'expect' anywhere in your code.
